executive_summary: LIMITED AWARENESS: The Monroe Police Department website contains 13 mentions of autism, disability, or accessibility-related terms. While some awareness exists, there is significant room for improvement in disability inclusion and training.

advocacy_implications.implication: Complete lack of preparedness for autistic community members
advocacy_implications.evidence: No mentions of autism, autistic, or autism spectrum across entire website
advocacy_implications.impact: High risk of miscommunication during police interactions

advocacy_implications.implication: No training or protocols for disability interactions
advocacy_implications.evidence: Zero mentions of disability, disabilities, or disabled persons

advocacy_implications.implication: Lack of understanding of legal requirements
advocacy_implications.evidence: No mentions of ADA or Americans with Disabilities Act

recommendations: Implement comprehensive autism awareness training for all Monroe Police officers
Develop clear protocols for traffic stops involving autistic individuals
Create accessible communication materials and procedures
Establish partnerships with local autism organizations in Monroe/Union County
Implement the Blue Envelope Program as the first structured approach to autism awareness
Develop ADA compliance training and procedures
Create community outreach programs for the autism community
